reason: Response A has the hallmarks of genuine Haiku output: an explicit topic-sentence intro/conclusion frame, comprehensive but evenly-weighted paragraphs, hedged general claims ("matters more than people realize"), and a safe, balanced register without sharp specificity. Response B is more distinctive and writerly, with crisp aphoristic openers ("Light matters most," "Clutter is stimulation"), precise mechanistic claims (sound decay time, cortical bypass, "below 70 degrees"), and a deliberately rhetorical closing line, which reads more like a stronger model imitating a clean style than Haiku's characteristically thorough, slightly generic prose.
